programs.course_name: Bachelor of Business (Honours) in Entrepreneurship and Innovation

course_fee.note: Fees may vary depending on units chosen and are subject to annual review.

equity_scholarship_scheme.description: A scholarship for low-income students studying in any faculty.

international_merit_scholarship.description: High achievement scholarship for future international undergraduate and postgraduate students.

related_courses.name: Bachelor of Business (Honours) in Human Resource Management

course_overview.description: The Bachelor of Business (Honours) in Entrepreneurship and Innovation is designed for students who wish to pursue a career in business with an emphasis on innovation, creativity, and entrepreneurship. The course provides advanced knowledge and skills in managing innovative projects, developing new ventures, and understanding the entrepreneurial mindset.
learning_outcomes: Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Understand the principles of entrepreneurship and innovation
Apply theoretical concepts to real-world business scenarios
Gain practical experience through internships or industry placements

english_language_proficiency.score_required: Overall band score of 6.5 with no individual band less than 6.0
Total score of 90, with minimum scores of 20 in Listening and Reading, 21 in Speaking, and 23 in Writing

academic_requirements.gpa_required: Minimum GPA of 5.0 on a 7-point scale

english_language_proficiency.score_required: Overall band score of 6.5 with no individual band less than 6.0
Total score of 90, with minimum scores of 20 in Listening and Reading, 21 in Speaking, and 23 in Writing

academic_requirements.gpa_required: Minimum GPA of 5.0 on a 7-point scale

one minor and 48 credit points chosen from unit options (electives)

course_overview.description: The Bachelor of Creative Arts (Acting) is a three-year full-time program offered at the Kelvin Grove campus. The course requires students to complete a successful audition and have sound knowledge in English, Literature, or related subjects.

additional_information.title: Assumed Knowledge
content: English, or Literature, or English and Literature Extension, or English as an Additional Language (Units 3 & 4, C)

additional_information.title: Important Dates
content: Applications and audition submissions close: 5pm Tuesday, 6 October 2026 (Brisbane time UTC+10)

steps.description: Apply for the Bachelor of Creative Arts (Acting) through QTAC.
Create an account with Acceptd and submit audition material including a profile photo, resume, monologue video, and duologue video.

exemptions: Applicants of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage
QTAC Educational Access Scheme (financial hardship category)

instructions.title: Read the Bachelor of Creative Arts (Acting) Audition Pieces (PDF file, 1.55 KB)
instructions.content: Follow instructions for choosing a script and recording your audition videos.

instructions.title: Watch How to Record Your QUT Acting Audition
instructions.content: This short video guides you through how to record your audition videos at home.

programs.course_duration: 1.5 years full-time or equivalent part-time study

programs.academic_requirements: A recognised bachelor degree (or higher) in any discipline with a minimum grade point average of 4.00 (on QUT’s 7 point scale). For the 1.5-year program: A recognised bachelor degree (or higher) in business or a related discipline with a minimum GPA of 4.00, or at least seven years full-time work experience.

programs.academic_requirements: A recognised bachelor degree in any field with a minimum grade point average (GPA) of 4.50 (on QUT's 7-point scale) and a minimum three years full-time post-degree work experience with at least two years relevant professional work experience and one year supervisory/management experience, or a recognised postgraduate degree in any field with the same GPA requirement and similar work experience.
programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: A recognised bachelor degree in any field with a minimum grade point average (GPA) of 4.50 (on QUT's 7-point scale) and a minimum three years full-time post-degree work experience with at least two years relevant professional work experience and one year supervisory/management experience, or a recognised postgraduate degree in any field with the same GPA requirement and similar work experience.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

programs.academic_requirements: You must have one of the following: A recognised bachelor degree (or higher) in any field; Completed the QUT Graduate Certificate in Digital Communication; or a recognised bachelor degree (or higher qualification) in a relevant discipline; or a recognised bachelor degree (or higher qualification) in any discipline followed by at least six months full-time (or equivalent) relevant professional experience.

Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

programs.academic_requirements: A completed recognised four-year full time (or equivalent) bachelor degree in engineering with a minimum grade point average (GPA) score of 4.00 (on QUT's 7-point scale); or A completed recognised three-year full-time (or equivalent) bachelor degree in engineering discipline with a minimum grade point average (GPA) score of 4.00 (on QUT's 7-point scale) plus at least two years full-time (or equivalent) professional engineering work experience.
programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: A completed recognised four-year full time (or equivalent) bachelor degree in engineering with a minimum grade point average (GPA) score of 4.00 (on QUT's 7-point scale); or A completed recognised three-year full-time (or equivalent) bachelor degree in engineering discipline with a minimum grade point average (GPA) score of 4.00 (on QUT's 7-point scale) plus at least two years full-time (or equivalent) professional engineering work experience.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.
